campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies in town have to pay. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to the city code, and council members discussed and debated the new rates before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for fiscal year 2026
Changes to Campbell Municipal Code regarding business taxes
Finance department presentation on annual tax rate updates
Council deliberation and vote on new tax provisions

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with the proposal and skipped the usual first reading process to speed things up. The council instructed staff to finalize the ordinance so it can be voted on and approved at the next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance fast-tracked for approval
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances for February, and authorize several construction and professional service contracts. The council gave the Town Manager permission to move forward with multiple construction projects and service agreements to keep city operations running.
keyTopics: Approval of February 2026 financial reports and investment activity
New construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other service providers
Town infrastructure improvements being planned
Three-year professional services agreement authorized

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 28, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several items including allowing free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for a community luncheon event. The council also discussed pay and contract agreements with park rangers, security initiatives with the Bay Area, and rules about affordable housing protections.
keyTopics: Free use of city rotunda approved for 'Together San José' community luncheon
New pay and contract terms agreed with park rangers
Bay Area security initiative coordination with the city
Affordable housing protections and what happens if agreements expire

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a meeting to review a draft plan for improving biodiversity and protecting trees throughout Mountain View. They also recognized the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center for receiving a state award and proclaimed April as Fair Housing Month in the city.
keyTopics: Draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan for community review
State recognition award for Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center
Fair Housing Month proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comments.
keyTopics: Interviews for open board and commission positions
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online participation options available

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The committee approved meeting minutes from January and reviewed how the city's retirement and healthcare benefit trust funds performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on city matters
Approval of January 26 meeting minutes
Retirement and healthcare benefit fund performance (Q1 2026)
State law limits on making decisions about items not listed on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also addressed a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one item was withdrawn by staff before a vote.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (Local 1171)
One agenda item withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to discuss several important financial and development matters. They held public hearings on tax rules, the next year's budget for development projects, and user fees, while also hearing about the county's plan to address homelessness in the community.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development budget approval
Updates to user fees and charges
County homelessness prevention initiative
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a decision about removing protected trees at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at a tree removal permit that was already approved through a simplified approval process.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit for 751 Waverley Street
Request for Director's Review to reconsider an approved permit
Over-the-Counter Planning process (simplified approval pathway)
Appeal or reconsideration of tree protection decision
